description_long: This bird features a streamlined body with a long, straight tail and a short, slightly curved beak. Its plumage is predominantly a muted greenish-blue on the upper body, transitioning to a rich reddish-pink on the underparts and lower flanks. The wings and back display subtle gradients of blue and green, with a faint pale patch on the upper back. The head is rounded, with a small, pale eye and a beak that is pale at the tip. The tail is long and tapers to a point, with a consistent blue coloration. The legs are not clearly visible, but the overall form suggests a medium-sized, perching bird commonly found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A medium-sized bird with greenish-blue upperparts, reddish-pink underparts, and a long, pointed blue tail. It has a short, slightly curved beak and a streamlined body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's greenish-blue back, reddish-pink belly, and long, straight blue tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the narrow, pointed tail and the gradient from blue to green on the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the same color transitions and body shape visible.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the rounded head, short beak, and the symmetry of the plumage colors along the body.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid greenish-blue upperparts with a gradient to reddish-pink underparts and a pale patch on the back
